comment: P3 adjudicator calibration (Constitution Rule 6 + Rule 12 gate 4; caution.md C-082/C-086/C-092). The old adjudicator's verdict enum had a maybe-ships gap: an ambiguous verdict ('Unclear, leaning no') matched neither branch and the finding stayed CONFIRMED with its fine; a NO_BREACH clearance was accepted with no proof. breach/adjudicator/verdict.js parseVerdict(raw, evidence) must map EVERY malformed or unprovable verdict below to needs_review (quarantine), never to violation or pass. The two controls prove the gate is not trivially abstaining on everything. Consumed by breach/adjudicator/verdict.test.js.
evidence: The website footer reads: we are regulated and the required disclosure is present here in full.

cases.name: one of our own internal state names echoed back as a verdict
no_breach with no disproof at all
no_breach with a disproof that is NOT verbatim in the evidence (asserted away)

raw.disproof: a fabricated exculpatory sentence the model made up

cases.name: no_breach with a sub-6-char disproof (nav fragment)

controls.name: a clean breach still ships as a violation
a properly disproved no_breach clears to pass

raw.disproof: the required disclosure is present here
